tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible impaired kidney function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may indicate renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome, diabetic nephropathy) or urinary tract issues. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Recommend clinical correlation for possible renal disease, review of diabetes status, and further nephrological/urological evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
